item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to document the retry-timeout mismatch fix in the Kafka consumer so future work does not repeat this bug.
special_constraints: Treat this as a verified solved lesson.
The result should become a searchable docs/solutions entry.
success_definition: Frames spin as writing or refreshing a docs/solutions entry.
Preserves the retrieval contract through frontmatter such as files_touched, tags, or doc_status.
Makes future implementation or debugging easier.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to refresh docs/solutions/workflow-issues/test-fixture-setup-2026-04-18.md based on what we learned today.
special_constraints: Prefer refreshing the existing doc over creating a duplicate.
Call out metadata refresh such as last_updated, tags, or files_touched.
success_definition: Prefers updating the named existing doc.
Does not drift into duplicate creation without justification.
Keeps the knowledge store coherent.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to capture the new Playwright setup guidance that replaces our older global-install recommendation.
special_constraints: This is a replacement case, not just a related note.
The older guidance should not remain as equal current truth.
success_definition: Uses supersede or canonical language rather than simple linking only.
Marks the older guidance as historical and points to the new doc.
Keeps the new guidance as the active answer.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to capture the lesson that Flywheel should present shape -> work -> review -> commit as the visible project-development loop and only offer spin after commit when there is durable value.
special_constraints: Treat this as a verified project-workflow correction from a completed session.
The result should become a searchable docs/solutions entry.
success_definition: Frames spin as writing or refreshing a docs/solutions entry.
Treats the workflow correction as durable project guidance rather than generic reflection.
Keeps the retrieval contract through frontmatter such as files_touched, tags, or doc_status.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin with no argument after a session that fixed a flaky retry bug and clarified our cache invalidation testing approach.
special_constraints: Blank input should trigger candidate discovery.
Offer a small candidate set rather than writing immediately.
success_definition: Produces a small bounded set of candidate lessons.
Asks which one to spin or whether to spin all.
Does not fabricate a giant backlog of lessons.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to capture a durable structured logging lesson for this repo.
special_constraints: Each spin should include housekeeping, but it must stay local and bounded.
Do not imply a full-store cleanup pass.
success_definition: Mentions neighboring-doc housekeeping or overlap cleanup.
Keeps the cleanup bounded to the strongest overlaps.
Avoids broad repo gardening.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:spin to capture the lesson that this repo should prefer right-sized bounded contexts, avoid speculative repositories, and run a bounded simplify pass after abstraction-heavy changes.
special_constraints: Treat this as durable architecture and code-quality guidance rather than a bug fix.
success_definition: Frames spin as durable repo guidance.
Preserves the retrieval contract through frontmatter or metadata awareness.
Makes the lesson useful to future planning, work, or review runs.
